seo.title: Trang: Complete Travel Guide 2026 — Emerald Cave, Islands & Dim Sum Breakfasts
seo.metaDescription: Plan your Trang trip with our complete 2026 guide. Emerald Cave on Koh Mook, Koh Kradan beaches, Hat Chao Mai National Park, dim sum breakfasts, and practical tips for Thailand's most underrated Andaman province.

overview: Trang is southern Thailand's best-kept secret — an Andaman province with stunning islands and zero of Krabi or Phuket's over-tourism. The Emerald Cave (Tham Morakot) on Koh Mook requires swimming through a pitch-dark 80-meter tunnel to emerge in a hidden lagoon surrounded by cliffs — one of the most theatrical experiences in all of Thailand. Nearby Koh Kradan is regularly voted Thailand's most beautiful beach, yet remains largely undeveloped thanks to its location inside Hat Chao Mai National Park. But Trang isn't just its islands: the province is as famous for its food culture as its beaches. Every morning, locals queue at shophouse restaurants along Thanon Ratchadamnoen for dim sum and moo yang (roast pork) — a unique Hokkien-Chinese breakfast tradition that dates back generations. Trang Province remains genuinely off the beaten path: the islands see a fraction of Koh Phi Phi's crowds, the roast pork costs 80 THB a portion, and you'll share longtail boats with Thai day-trippers rather than tour-group crowds.
highlights: Emerald Cave (Tham Morakot) on Koh Mook — swim through an 80-meter pitch-dark sea cave to emerge in a hidden white-sand lagoon surrounded by towering cliffs
Koh Kradan — voted one of Thailand's top beaches, nearly undeveloped, inside Hat Chao Mai National Park (200 THB entrance)
Trang dim sum breakfast culture — queue at Ruanthai Dim Sum or Phong Ocha on Thanon Ratchadamnoen for Hokkien-style dim sum and roast pork from 6 AM
Moo yang (Trang roast pork) — whole pigs marinated 8 hours in Chinese spices and roasted; the crispy pork belly is served over rice or with dim sum (80-120 THB)
Hat Chao Mai National Park — 231 km² of protected coastline, mangroves, seagrass beds, and 5 islands including Koh Mook and Koh Kradan
Koh Libong — Thailand's largest dugong sanctuary, with boat tours to spot dugongs grazing in the seagrass
Cave kayaking at Le Khaw Kob — paddle through limestone cave networks along underground streams in an extraordinary karst landscape
Trang's night market — a lively evening market on Thanon Wiset Kunthorn with southern Thai street food at genuine local prices (30-80 THB)
Koh Ngai (Koh Hai) — long white beach on a small island 1.5 hours from Trang Pier, accessible by ferry and popular for snorkeling
Koh Cheuk & Koh Waen — small uninhabited islands near Koh Kradan for excellent snorkeling in crystal-clear shallow water

bestTimeToVisit.peak: Nov-Apr: Dry season on the Andaman side, calm seas, clear visibility for snorkeling. December-February is peak with best beach conditions on all islands. January is ideal for combining dim sum culture with island hopping.
bestTimeToVisit.shoulder: Oct-Nov & Apr-May: Transition months with occasionally rough seas but fewer visitors and lower prices. April-May is hot (35C+) but the islands are still accessible most days.
bestTimeToVisit.lowSeason: May-Oct: Southwest monsoon brings heavy rain and rough seas. Ferry services to Koh Mook, Koh Kradan, and Koh Ngai are reduced or suspended June-August. Trang Town itself is fine year-round — the dim sum culture, caves, and inland attractions are unaffected by the monsoon.
bestTimeToVisit.recommendation: Visit November to April for full island access and Emerald Cave tours. January is the sweet spot — dry weather, calm seas, and the islands are at their most beautiful. Avoid planning island trips July-September when ferry services are frequently cancelled. Trang Town and cave tours operate year-round regardless of sea conditions.

topAttractions.name: Emerald Cave (Tham Morakot), Koh Mook
topAttractions.description: Thailand's most dramatic natural experience — a sea cave with an 80-meter dark tunnel that you must swim through (torch provided, or enter by kayak) to reach a hidden inland beach. The lagoon inside is surrounded on all sides by towering limestone cliffs with a small white-sand beach accessible only via this cave. Tour boats arrive 10 AM-2 PM for the best light inside the lagoon.
topAttractions.location: Koh Mook, Hat Chao Mai National Park, 1.5 hours from Trang Pier by speedboat
tips: Visit early (before 10 AM) or after 2 PM to avoid tour boat crowds
Carry nothing — leave bags on the boat and swim with just your phone in a waterproof case
The tide matters: visit at mid-tide for the easiest passage — low tide exposes rocks, high tide floods the tunnel

topAttractions.name: Koh Kradan Beach
topAttractions.description: Consistently ranked among Thailand's best beaches — a long, gently curving stretch of white sand on a small island entirely within Hat Chao Mai National Park. The reef running along the beach's east side has exceptional snorkeling with healthy coral and tropical fish visible in crystal-clear water. With limited accommodation and no roads, the island retains an untouched quality that Koh Phi Phi lost decades ago.
topAttractions.location: Koh Kradan, Hat Chao Mai National Park, 1.5 hours from Trang Pier by speedboat
tips: Bring your own snorkel gear — rentals are limited on the island
Stay overnight at one of the eco-resorts for the island to yourself in the morning and evening
The west-facing beach has the best sunset views in Trang Province

topAttractions.name: Trang Dim Sum Breakfast
topAttractions.description: Trang's most celebrated cultural experience — the daily morning dim sum ritual that has made this city famous throughout Thailand. Since the late 19th century, Hokkien-Chinese immigrants created a breakfast culture unique to Trang: dozens of shophouses along Thanon Ratchadamnoen open at 5-6 AM serving bamboo steamer stacks of ha gao, siu mai, and char siu bao alongside moo yang (roast pork). The signature condiment is kam chueang — a coral-red sauce made from cassava, vinegar, and spices unique to Trang.
topAttractions.entranceFee: Free (dim sum 15-25 THB per piece, full breakfast 100-200 THB)
tips: Go between 6-8 AM for the freshest selection — popular spots like Ruanthai Dim Sum sell out by 10 AM
Order the pork belly moo yang alongside your dim sum — the combination is Trang's definitive breakfast
The kam chueang dipping sauce is unique to Trang — each restaurant makes their own version

topAttractions.name: Hat Chao Mai National Park
topAttractions.description: A 231 km² marine national park protecting 5 islands (including Koh Mook and Koh Kradan), 40 km of mainland coastline, mangrove forests, and seagrass beds. The park is one of Thailand's most important dugong habitats — around 100 dugongs graze the seagrass near Koh Libong. Wildlife includes sea turtles, monitor lizards, fishing eagles, and a variety of reef fish. The mainland section has tidal mangrove trails and a visitor center near Ban Chao Mai.
topAttractions.location: Ban Chao Mai (park HQ), 38 km south of Trang City
tips: The 200 THB fee covers all park islands on the same day — keep your ticket
Ban Chao Mai pier is the main departure point for longtail boats to the islands
Ask at the park HQ about dugong spotting boat tours to Koh Libong (seasonal, ~500 THB)

topAttractions.name: Le Khaw Kob Cave (Tham Le Khaw Kob)
topAttractions.description: A spectacular cave system where a boat carries you along an underground river through a single limestone mountain. The river flows through chambers hung with massive stalactites and stalagmites — glowing orange and white in torchlight. Unlike crowded tourist caves, Tham Le Khaw Kob feels genuinely wild. Local guides navigate the river by boat, stopping at chambers to let you explore on foot.
topAttractions.location: Khao Kob, Huay Yod District, 30 km north of Trang City
topAttractions.entranceFee: 200 THB boat tour (~$6 USD) plus guide fee
tips: Go in the morning when the light penetrates the cave entrance and illuminates the formations
The cave is flood-prone after heavy rain — check conditions before visiting in the wet season
The approach road passes through beautiful karst countryside worth stopping for photos

topAttractions.name: Koh Libong
topAttractions.description: Thailand's largest island in the Andaman Sea, and the country's most important dugong sanctuary with around 100 dugongs resident year-round. The island is largely undeveloped, with fishing villages, mangroves, and extensive seagrass beds. Boat tours to spot dugongs depart from the main pier and run most days in calm weather. The island also has a bird sanctuary where raptors and sea eagles are commonly spotted.
topAttractions.location: Koh Libong, 40 km from Trang City (ferry from Ban Chao Mai or Kantang)
tips: Dawn boat tours have the best dugong sightings — the animals feed actively in morning light
Stay overnight at one of the eco-lodges for the full wildlife experience
Combine with a Koh Kradan or Koh Mook visit by private longtail (negotiate ~1,500-2,000 THB for the day)

travelTips: Trang City is the logistics hub — most guesthouses are near the train station on Thanon Phra Ram VI. Use it as your base for day trips to all islands and caves
The Trang dim sum breakfast culture starts at 5-6 AM — if you're on an island tour departing at 8 AM, you can still squeeze in a full dim sum breakfast
Ban Chao Mai pier (38 km south of Trang City) is the departure point for speedboats to Hat Chao Mai National Park islands — arrange your hotel or guesthouse to organize transport
Hat Chao Mai National Park entrance (200 THB) covers all island visits in a single day — keep your ticket stub
Trang is significantly less touristy than Krabi — you'll share longtail boats and beach restaurants with Thai day-trippers, not package tours
Trang train station has connections to Bangkok (Hua Lamphong, ~12-14 hours overnight) and Hat Yai — the overnight sleeper train is a good budget option
ATMs are clustered around the train station area and Tesco Lotus on Thanon Pha Ram VI — withdraw larger amounts to minimize fees (220 THB/withdrawal)
The best island combination for a single day: Emerald Cave (Koh Mook) + Koh Kradan + snorkeling at Koh Cheuk (covered by 3-island tour operators, ~1,500-2,000 THB)
gettingThere: Trang Airport (TST) has daily flights from Bangkok (AirAsia, Nok Air) from 1,800 THB one-way (1.5 hours). Airport to city center is 15 minutes by taxi (~250 THB) or songtheaw (50 THB). From Bangkok by train: overnight sleeper from Hua Lamphong Station (~14 hours, 500-1,200 THB depending on class). From Krabi: 1.5 hours by minivan (250 THB). From Satun/Koh Lipe: 2 hours by minivan (300 THB). From Hat Yai: 2 hours by bus or minivan (180-250 THB).
gettingAround: Trang City is compact and walkable from the train station area. Motorbike taxis are available around the train station (40-80 THB for short trips). Renting a motorbike (200-300 THB/day) gives freedom to reach Ban Chao Mai pier and cave attractions. Songthaew (shared trucks) run between Trang City and major points south for 40-80 THB. For island trips, speedboats depart Ban Chao Mai pier — tours include transport from Trang City hotels (~1,500-2,000 THB/person). Grab does not reliably operate in Trang — use motorbike taxis or arrange private car hire (800-1,200 THB/day).
